description_long: This is a small, fixed-wing airplane with a sleek, streamlined fuselage and a high-wing configuration. The wings are straight with red-tipped ends and red panels on the upper surfaces, and the tail features a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and red accents. The cockpit is located at the front of the fuselage, featuring multiple windows for visibility, and appears to be enclosed and likely seats two to four occupants. The airplane is equipped with a single propeller engine mounted at the nose, and the landing gear is a tricycle type with two main wheels and a nose wheel, all fixed and exposed. The overall color scheme is primarily light gray with distinct red highlights on the wingtips, tail, and nose. The design suggests a general aviation or light utility aircraft suitable for short-range flights.
description: A small, high-wing airplane with a single nose-mounted propeller, red-tipped wings, and a conventional tail, finished in light gray with red accents.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the nose-mounted propeller, cockpit windows, and red accents on the wingtips and tail surfaces.
description_view_1: Rear-left view highlighting the high-wing structure, red upper wing panels, and the exposed landing gear.
description_view_2: Rear-right view displaying the tail section, red-tipped wings, and the single vertical stabilizer with red highlights.
description_view_3: Front-right view focusing on the cockpit, nose propeller, and the red accents on the nose and tail.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight wings with slight dihedral and red-tipped ends; no winglets or variable geometry.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers; red accents on control surfaces.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed tricycle landing gear with two main wheels and a single nose wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple side windows, likely accommodating two to four seats.
